author: research-shell-nix
content:
Decision: Delivered a working shell.nix (verified inside crosslink-sandbox) rather than recommending only one path.

The shell.nix supports TWO modes:
1. **Preferred: .conda-env prefix** — full conda env fidelity, requires one-time setup outside sandbox
2. **Fallback: pure nix python314** — works out of the box, covers most agent tasks, missing 5 packages (gcsfs, s3fs, domain-utils, tranco, pytest-split)

Also documented a proposed ~25-line sandbox change (.sandbox.json) for full conda support as a follow-up.

Chose this over pure nix-only because: conda env has 185 packages with exact version pinning — replicating that entirely in nix would be fragile and a maintenance burden.
Chose this over conda-only because: conda binary isn't available in sandbox without modification, and the nix fallback works for most tasks today.
